name: TAO Compression Fakeout Reexpansion v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing compression fakeout into re-expansion with directional confirmation. The edge should come from harvesting the move after the trap is revealed, not from taking raw first breaks.
invalidation_condition: Abort if compression-plus-fakeout stays too sparse, if fakeout and reclaim steps overconstrain participation, or if re-expansion fails to persist after the trap.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Volatility compresses, price briefly probes one side without acceptance, then expands through the opposite side once the fakeout is rejected.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Choppy environments often create false starts before the cleaner directional leg. Waiting for the trap-and-release sequence can offer better asymmetry than chasing the first break.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is useful because fakeout/re-expansion behavior can be more expressive on a volatile asset while still staying in a supported lane.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If compression resolves directly instead of faking out first, this logic may still be too restrictive.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the trap logic engages but follow-through is short, simplify exits before changing the fakeout definition.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: acceptable; compression, probe, and reclaim are staged rather than redundant
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed with moderate compression thresholds and broad fakeout rejection logic
